message:
feat(config): Implement hot-reload support for runtime configuration

Add infrastructure for safe configuration hot-reloading without daemon
restart. The system distinguishes between settings that can be changed
at runtime (poll intervals, retention, pattern packs) and those that
require restart (db_path, data_dir, pool sizes).

New types:
- HotReloadableConfig: Extracts the subset of Config that can be
  safely applied at runtime without reinitializing stateful components.
- HotReloadResult: Comparison result showing allowed changes vs
  forbidden changes that would require restart.
- HotReloadChange: Individual setting change with old/new values.
- ForbiddenChange: Blocked change with explanation of why restart
  is required.

Config methods:
- diff_for_hot_reload(): Compare configs and categorize changes.
- hot_reloadable(): Extract HotReloadableConfig from full Config.

Hot-reloadable settings include: log_level, poll_interval_ms,
min_poll_interval_ms, retention_days, retention_max_mb,
checkpoint_interval_secs, pattern_packs, workflows_enabled,
auto_run_allowlist.

Forbidden settings: storage.db_path, general.data_dir,
storage.writer_queue_size, storage.read_pool_size.

Comprehensive test suite validates allowed changes, forbidden changes,
mixed scenarios, and Display formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
